sections.label: Section 1585
sections.original: αὑτοῦ δ [*] ἀδελφόν , ἀμφίλεκτος ὢν κράτει ,
sections.translation: and his own brother . And when he had come back as a suppliant to his hearth , unhappy Thyestes secured such safety for his lot as not himself to suffer death and stain with his blood his native soil .

sections.label: Section 1590
sections.original: [ αὐτοῦ ] ξένια δὲ τοῦδε δύσθεος πατὴρ
sections.translation: But Atreus , the godless father of this slain man , with welcome more hearty than kind , on the pretence that he was cheerfully celebrating a happy day by serving meat , served up to my father as entertainment a banquet of his own children ’ s flesh .

sections.label: Section 1595
sections.original: ἔθρυπτ ἄνωθεν ἀνδρακὰς καθήμενος [*]
sections.translation: The toes and fingers he broke off ⋯ sitting apart . [*] And when all unwittingly my father had quickly taken servings that he did not recognize , he ate a meal which , as you see , has proved fatal to his race . Now , discovering his unhallowed deed , he uttered a great cry , reeled back , vomiting forth the slaughtered flesh , and invoked

sections.label: Section 1600
sections.original: μόρον δ ἄφερτον Πελοπίδαις ἐπεύχεται ,
sections.translation: an unbearable curse upon the line of Pelops , kicking the banquet table to aid his curse , thus perish all the race of Pleisthenes ! This is the reason that you see this man fallen here . I am he who planned this murder and with justice . For together with my hapless father he drove me out ,

sections.label: Section 1605
sections.original: τρίτον γὰρ ὄντα μ ἐπὶ δυσαθλίῳ [*] πατρὶ
sections.translation: me his third child , as yet a baby in swaddling-clothes . But grown to manhood , justice has brought me back again . Exile though I was , I laid my hand upon my enemy , compassing every device of cunning to his ruin .

sections.label: Section 1610
sections.original: οὕτω καλὸν δὴ καὶ τὸ κατθανεῖν ἐμοί ,
sections.translation: So even death would be sweet to me now that I behold him in justice ’ s net .

sections.label: Section 1612
sections.original: Αἴγισθ , ὑβρίζειν ἐν κακοῖσιν [*] οὐ σέβω .
sections.translation: Aegisthus , excessive triumph amid distress I do not honor . You say that of your own intent you slew this man and did alone plot this pitiful murder .

sections.label: Section 1615
sections.original: οὔ φημ ἀλύξειν ἐν δίκῃ τὸ σὸν κάρα
sections.translation: I tell you in the hour of justice that you yourself , be sure of that , will not escape the people ’ s curses and death by stoning at their hand .

sections.label: Section 1617
sections.original: σὺ ταῦτα φωνεῖς νερτέρᾳ προσήμενος
sections.translation: You speak like that , you who sit at the lower oar when those upon the higher bench control the ship ? [*] Old as you are , you shall learn how bitter it is

sections.label: Section 1620
sections.original: τῷ τηλικούτῳ , σωφρονεῖν εἰρημένον .
sections.translation: at your age to be schooled when prudence is the lesson set before you . Bonds and the pangs of hunger are far the best doctors of the spirit when it comes to instructing the old . Do you have eyes and lack understanding ? Do not kick against the goads lest you strike to your own hurt .

sections.label: Section 1625
sections.original: γύναι , σὺ τοὺς ἥκοντας ἐκ μάχης μένων [*]
sections.translation: Woman that you are ! Skulking at home and awaiting the return of the men from war , all the while defiling a hero ’ s bed , did you contrive this death against a warrior chief ?

sections.label: Section 1628
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτα τἄπη κλαυμάτων ἀρχηγενῆ .
sections.translation: These words of yours likewise shall prove a source of tears . The tongue of Orpheus is quite the opposite of yours .

sections.label: Section 1630
sections.original: ὁ μὲν γὰρ ἦγε πάντ ἀπὸ φθογγῆς χαρᾷ ,
sections.translation: He led all things by the rapture of his voice ; but you , who have stirred our wrath by your silly yelping , shall be led off yourself . You will appear tamer when put down by force .

sections.label: Section 1633
sections.original: ὡς δὴ σύ μοι τύραννος Ἀργείων ἔσει ,
sections.translation: As if you would ever truly be my master here in Argos , you who did contrive our king ’ s death , and

sections.label: Section 1635
sections.original: δρᾶσαι τόδ ἔργον οὐκ ἔτλης αὐτοκτόνως .
sections.translation: then had not the courage to do this deed of murder with your own hand !

sections.label: Section 1636
sections.original: τὸ γὰρ δολῶσαι πρὸς γυναικὸς ἦν σαφῶς :
sections.translation: Because to ensnare him was clearly the woman ’ s part ; I was suspect as his enemy of old . However , with his gold I shall endeavor to control the people ; and whoever is unruly ,

sections.label: Section 1640
sections.original: ζεύξω βαρείαις οὔτι μοι [*] σειραφόρον
sections.translation: him I ’ ll yoke with a heavy collar , and in truth he shall be no well-fed trace-horse ! [*] No ! Loathsome hunger that houses with darkness shall see him gentle .

sections.label: Section 1643
sections.original: τί δὴ τὸν ἄνδρα τόνδ ἀπὸ ψυχῆς κακῆς
sections.translation: Why then , in the baseness of your soul , did you not kill him yourself , but leave his slaying to a woman ,

sections.label: Section 1645
sections.original: χώρας μίασμα καὶ θεῶν ἐγχωρίων
sections.translation: a plague to her country and her country ’ s gods ? Oh , does Orestes perhaps still behold the light , that , with favoring fortune , he may come home and be the slayer of this pair with victory complete ?

sections.label: Section 1649
sections.original: ἀλλ ἐπεὶ δοκεῖς τάδ ἔρδειν καὶ λέγειν , γνώσει τάχα .
sections.translation: Oh well , since you plan to act and speak like that , you shall be taught a lesson soon .

sections.label: Section 1650
sections.original: εἶα δή , φίλοι λοχῖται , τοὔργον οὐχ ἑκὰς τόδε . [*]
sections.translation: On guard , my trusty guardsmen , your work lies close to hand .

sections.label: Section 1651
sections.original: εἶα δή , ξίφος πρόκωπον πᾶς τις εὐτρεπιζέτω .
sections.translation: On guard then ! Let every one make ready his sword with hand on hilt .

sections.label: Section 1652
sections.original: ἀλλὰ μὴν κἀγὼ πρόκωπος οὐκ ἀναίνομαι θανεῖν .
sections.translation: My hand too is laid on my sword hilt , and I do not shrink from death .

sections.label: Section 1653
sections.original: δεχομένοις λέγεις θανεῖν σε : τὴν τύχην δ αἱρούμεθα . [*]
sections.translation: Death for yourself , you say . We hail the omen . We welcome fortune ’ s test .

sections.label: Section 1654
sections.original: μηδαμῶς , ὦ φίλτατ ἀνδρῶν , ἄλλα δράσωμεν [*] κακά .
sections.translation: No , my dearest , let us work no further ills . Even these are many to reap , a wretched harvest . Of woe we have enough ; let us have no bloodshed . Venerable elders , go back to your homes , and yield in time to destiny before you come to harm . What we did had to be done . But should this trouble prove enough , we will accept it ,

sections.label: Section 1655
sections.original: ἀλλὰ καὶ τάδ ἐξαμῆσαι πολλὰ δύστηνον θέρος : [*]
sections.translation: But to think that these men should let their wanton tongues thus blossom into speech against me and cast about such insults , putting their fortune to the test ! To reject wise counsel and insult their master !

sections.label: Section 1660
sections.original: δαίμονος χηλῇ βαρείᾳ δυστυχῶς πεπληγμένοι .
sections.translation: sorely battered as we are by the heavy hand of fate . Such is a woman ’ s counsel , if any care to learn from it .

sections.label: Section 1662
sections.original: ἀλλὰ τούσδ ἐμοὶ [*] ματαίαν γλῶσσαν ὧδ ἀπανθίσαι
sections.translation: It would not be like men of Argos to cringe before a man as low as you .

sections.label: Section 1665
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν Ἀργείων τόδ εἴη , φῶτα προσσαίνειν κακόν .
sections.translation: Ha ! I will visit you with vengeance yet in days to come .

sections.label: Section 1666
sections.original: ἀλλ ἐγώ σ ἐν ὑστέραισιν ἡμέραις μέτειμ ἔτι .
sections.translation: Not if fate shall guide Orestes to return home .

sections.label: Section 1667
sections.original: οὔκ , ἐὰν δαίμων Ὀρέστην δεῦρ ἀπευθύνῃ μολεῖν .
sections.translation: From my own experience I know that exiles feed on hope .

sections.label: Section 1668
sections.original: οἶδ ἐγὼ φεύγοντας ἄνδρας ἐλπίδας σιτουμένους .
sections.translation: Keep on , grow fat , polluting justice , since you can .

sections.label: Section 1669
sections.original: πρᾶσσε , πιαίνου , μιαίνων τὴν δίκην , ἐπεὶ πάρα .
sections.translation: Know that you shall atone to me for your insolent folly .

sections.label: Section 1670
sections.original: ἴσθι μοι δώσων ἄποινα τῆσδε μωρίας χάριν .
sections.translation: Brag in your bravery like a cock beside his hen .

sections.label: Section 1671
sections.original: κόμπασον θαρσῶν , ἀλέκτωρ ὥστε [*] θηλείας πέλας .
sections.translation: Do no care for their idle yelpings . I and you will be masters of this house and order it aright .
